Transcrição Completa: 30 Bonus Mindset
[00:00] Welcome. This is the bonus mindset section. I understand the pain, the challenges, the love, the blood, the sweat, the tears, the effort, the energy, the sacrifice that it takes to really commit and scale an agency to the next level to make the kind of money you want to make to position it to exit for that big financial event.
[00:24] So what I want to share with you are a couple sections of mindset that I think are really important. So when it gets tough, you dig down and you really understand some of these things conceptually.
[00:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Counsel vs Advice"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Conceito comparativo entre conselho profissional e opinião não qualificada, representação visual de mentoria versus sugestões casuais
[00:37] So let's first start with this idea of counsel versus advice. Okay, right now you have a bunch of people surrounded that have great intentions in your life. It could be a brother, a sister, a mother, a grandparent, a loved one, a wife, a husband, a boyfriend, a girlfriend. These people care about you and they love you.
[00:57] And they're constantly trying to help you when you're in need or when you're down, hopefully, if you've built a great tribe. But the problem is if they haven't achieved what you want or more and they're giving you advice, it's simply that. It's just advice.
[01:13] What I would suggest that you do quickly is go seek out counsel, seek out mentorship. You should only be listening to people that have a certain skill set and knowledge that you don't possess and/or have achieved what you want or more in the field that you're seeking to do that in.
[01:30] So big big difference. It took me a long time to get that concept. I had to make a lot of big mistakes. But always seek counsel versus advice.
[01:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Fear vs Love"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Comparação entre estilos de gestão: liderança baseada em medo versus liderança baseada em confiança e amor, representação de gestão tirânica versus gestão empoderadora
[01:42] The next as you start to grow your agency, I want to tell you about the concept of fear versus love. It's two different ways to manage. If you're constantly, you know, from this kind of tyrant viewpoint and you're leading and trying to manage your people out of fear, you're never going to grow.
[02:00] You're never going to get them. You're never they're never going to feel empowered. You're never going to be out of this micromanagement lens until you get them to start to love you and start to trust you, right?
[02:11] You want to condition your team and empower your team to where if they let you down or other team members down, it's more disappointment versus fear. And that's a big concept. And especially when you get stressed, the difference between fear versus love from a management style.
[02:27] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Empowerment vs Micromanagement"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Conceito de delegação efetiva versus controle excessivo, representação de trabalhar NO negócio versus trabalhar NO negócio, ilustração de liderança que liberta versus liderança que sufoca
[02:29] Second, empowerment versus micromanagement. Once you get the right team and the right people in place and you clearly define what they should be doing and you've given them purpose, a good leader releases that leash and empowers the team and holds them accountable and trusts them to progress.
[02:50] Great leaders can't micromanage because they'll always be in working in the business versus working on the business. So think about what you're doing, what your style is right now. And if you're finding you have to constantly micromanage your team and you cannot empower them, then you probably have the wrong people.
[03:10] But you need to do this concept if you want to start to see any sort of scale.
[03:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Greed vs Scale"
NÚMEROS: 2 pessoas para 200 pessoas mencionados
VISUAL: Comparação entre ganhos de curto prazo versus investimento de longo prazo, representação de crescimento exponencial, ilustração de sacrifício financeiro temporário para crescimento massivo
[03:17] Next, I want to talk about the concept of greed versus scale. If you're just out to make a lot of money short-term and cash flow, that's there's nothing wrong with that. But if you're really looking to take it to the next level, you're looking for a major exit, you want to go from two people to 200 people, you're going to have to understand that it's going to take sacrifice financially and it's going to take investment.
[03:43] So you may there may be years you don't make as much as you're used to or there may be years where you're bonusing out people and you're hiring new people but you can't bonus yourself. You've got to be thinking of the bigger picture.
[03:56] Remember people are your biggest capital in this business. And if once you get in the mindset of scale versus greed it puts you in the position to take the company to the next level.
[04:07] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Management vs Leadership"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Contraste entre dar ordens versus liderar pelo exemplo, representação de "walk the talk", ilustração de líder trabalhando junto com a equipe versus gerente apenas delegando
[04:09] Management versus leadership. Okay, a manager will basically tell you what to do. A leader will lead through actions. So for years, I mean until I sold the company, I was the first person in the office and one of the last to leave.
[04:26] And that was simply because of my work ethic and what I want to do. And I'm not suggesting that everybody does that, but you lead through actions, right, versus barking orders. Big difference in concept.
[04:38] You need to have your team see that you're willing to roll up your sleeves and do what you're asking them to do. If you're going to make somebody work over the weekend, you need to be committed to leading the charge in that weekend effort.
[04:50] If you're constantly barking orders and you're all talk and no walk, there's going to be an issue. Difference between management and leadership.
[04:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sprint vs Marathon - Scale with Speed"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Conceito de velocidade versus lentidão, representação de urgência e decisões rápidas, ilustração do livro "Scale with Speed", ícones de tecnologia e oportunidades que fecham rapidamente
[05:00] The last concept here, and this is a super important one. I mean, it's the premise of why I wrote this whole book, Scale with Speed. You know the old saying, hey, slow down, little Billy. It's a marathon, not a sprint. Fuck that. It's a sprint.
[05:18] The minute that you think it's not a sprint, it's a marathon distance, but you need to be running. You need to move fast. You need to make decisions quickly. You need to deliver fast.
[05:29] Everything you need to do, especially in this space with technology and marketing, things are moving so fast and opportunity windows close even quicker. So, this idea of, hey, it's time to slow down. Hey, let's really think this through. You need to be making quick decisions and moving fast.
[05:47] I hope this helped. We'll have a Q&A section here at the end and I look forward to talking to you then.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Crescimento de 2 pessoas para 200 pessoas mencionado como meta de escala
Múltiplos anos de sacrifício financeiro necessários para crescimento
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Counsel vs Advice: Buscar mentoria de pessoas que já alcançaram o que você busca, não apenas conselhos de pessoas bem-intencionadas
Fear vs Love: Gestão baseada em confiança e empoderamento gera decepção saudável ao invés de medo paralisante
Empowerment vs Micromanagement: Liberar a equipe para trabalhar ON the business, não IN the business
Greed vs Scale: Sacrificar ganhos de curto prazo para investir em pessoas e crescimento exponencial
Management vs Leadership: Liderar pelo exemplo, ser o primeiro a chegar e último a sair, "walk the talk"
Sprint vs Marathon: Tomar decisões rápidas e agir com velocidade, especialmente em tecnologia e marketing
ESTRUTURA:
6 conceitos fundamentais de mindset para escalar agências
Foco em preparação para exit strategy e evento financeiro significativo
Ênfase em pessoas como maior capital do negócio
Metodologia "Scale with Speed" como filosofia central
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Anos de potencial redução de lucro pessoal para investimento em equipe
Bonificação de equipe prioritária sobre bonificação pessoal durante crescimento
Foco em grande exit financeiro ao invés de cash flow de curto prazo
DURAÇÃO: ~6 minutos FORMATO: Apresentação educacional direta para câmera, seção bônus de mindset
